## `scripts/lib/recipe-yaml.mjs`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`parseYaml`](<../../scripts/lib/recipe-yaml.mjs#L13>) | function | `export function parseYaml(text)` | Minimal block-YAML parser (no dependency) shared by the platform-loop tools. Supports exactly the subset the platform-loop recipes use: block mappings, block sequences of scalars, and block sequences of mappings, nested by indentation, with optionally quoted scalar values. Anything it cannot parse surfaces as a recipe gap rather than silently passing. This is intentionally the same grammar enforced by audit-platform-loops.mjs; the runtime runner reuses it so a recipe that the static guard accepts is the same document the runner plans/executes. |
| [`indentOf`](<../../scripts/lib/recipe-yaml.mjs#L21>) | function | `indentOf = (l) =>` | Implements indent of for repository automation. |
| [`parseScalar`](<../../scripts/lib/recipe-yaml.mjs#L23>) | function | `function parseScalar(v)` | Parses scalar for repository automation. |

| [`parseSeq`](<../../scripts/lib/recipe-yaml.mjs#L56>) | function | `function parseSeq(indent)` | Parses seq for repository automation. |

## `src-next/app/(control-panel)/flyto/callback/GitHubAppCallbackPage.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`Phase`](<../../src-next/app/(control-panel)/flyto/callback/GitHubAppCallbackPage.tsx#L9>) | type | `type Phase = 'working' \| 'done' \| 'error'` | Defines the phase contract used by the application. |
| [`GitHubAppCallbackPage`](<../../src-next/app/(control-panel)/flyto/callback/GitHubAppCallbackPage.tsx#L27>) | React component | `export function GitHubAppCallbackPage()` | GitHub App install callback. GitHub redirects here after the user completes the install flow at github.com/apps/{slug}/installations/new. Query string contract (set by GitHub): installation_id — the numeric installation_id we'll bind to the org setup_action — 'install' \| 'update' \| 'request' state — the orgID we passed when redirecting the user out (round-trips through GitHub unchanged) We POST {installation_id, setup_action} to /api/v1/code/orgs/{state}/github/connect — the backend re-validates the installation by minting a token and persists the org↔installation mapping in github_app_installations. The user is then bounced back to the repo picker or settings. |

## `src-next/app/(control-panel)/flyto/workspace/components/pages/WarRoomSectionPage.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`WarRoomSectionPage`](<../../src-next/app/(control-panel)/flyto/workspace/components/pages/WarRoomSectionPage.tsx#L35>) | React component | `export default function WarRoomSectionPage({ sectionKey }: { sectionKey: Section['id'] })` | WarRoomSectionPage — the modern home for the two technical surfaces that used to live in the legacy below-the-divider accordion: Architecture (arch-*) and Code Scans (sec-*). The accordion is gone; these are now first-class MODULES entries ('architecture' / 'code-scans') with a splat path. The deep section id rides in the URL splat — '/architecture/arch-deps' — so every technical view is individually deep-linkable. The render engine is still WarRoomView + sectionRegistry (we do NOT rewrite those deep views); this wrapper only supplies the inner sub-tab nav that the accordion used to provide. Bare '/architecture' (empty splat) falls back to the section's first item. Old '/warroom/arch-*' bookmarks still resolve via WarRoomDispatch (route.tsx), which forwards here. |
| [`onClick`](<../../src-next/app/(control-panel)/flyto/workspace/components/pages/WarRoomSectionPage.tsx#L141>) | object callback | `onClick: () => navigate('/projects/${orgId}/settings')` | Handles click for the user interface. |

| [`ExploreHomeView`](<../../src-next/app/(public)/(explore)/components/ExploreHomeView.tsx#L31>) | React component | `export default function ExploreHomeView()` | ExploreHomeView — public /explore landing page. Three blocks: 1. Hero with "we know N companies across M industries" badge 2. Industries grid — click into an industry to see its companies 3. Coverage map — per-country count + freshness pill Bypasses the logged-in workspace chrome entirely (ExploreLayout). Designed for organic search landings + share links — first paint must look intentional even before data arrives, hence the skeleton placeholders sized to the final layout. |

Each row is a public scorecard link; no grade is shown here (intentional — the teaser grade appears only on the per-domain page so the click- through has a payoff). |

## `src-next/app/(public)/(explore)/components/ExploreLayout.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`ExploreLayout`](<../../src-next/app/(public)/(explore)/components/ExploreLayout.tsx#L20>) | React component | `export default function ExploreLayout({ children }: { children: ReactNode })` | ExploreLayout — chrome for every public /explore/* page. Renders a minimal top bar (brand + sign-up button) above the page content. Deliberately distinct from the logged-in workspace shell so visitors don't get confused into thinking they're already in the product. Why a custom shell vs the FuseLayout chrome: the FuseLayout assumes an authenticated user (org switcher, avatar, notifications). Threading "anonymous mode" through Fuse's component tree would create more surface area for auth bugs than just rendering a separate shell. |

## `src-next/app/App.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`GlobalErrorListeners`](<../../src-next/app/App.tsx#L34>) | React component | `function GlobalErrorListeners()` | Global error listeners — mounted once inside SnackbarProvider so every unhandled mutation failure and auth expiry surfaces a toast. Also picks up 'flyto:entitlement-denied' events emitted by the engine client when the backend rejects a request with a capability error (feature_required / action_required / *_cap_exceeded). Centralising the routing here means no individual fetch call site has to know about toast wiring. |
| [`onMutErr`](<../../src-next/app/App.tsx#L37>) | function | `onMutErr = (e: Event) =>` | Handles mut err for the application. |

## `src-next/components/atoms/EmptyStateGuide.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`EmptyStateGuideStep`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/EmptyStateGuide.tsx#L7>) | type | `export type EmptyStateGuideStep = { label: string hint?: string }` | Defines the empty state guide step contract used by the user interface. |
| [`EmptyStateGuideProps`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/EmptyStateGuide.tsx#L12>) | type | `export type EmptyStateGuideProps = { /** lucide-react icon component, rendered at ~28px in a slate-tinted square */ icon?: ReactNode title: string /** One short paragraph; keep under ~120 chars so the empty state stays scannable. */ description?: string /** Op` | Defines the empty state guide props contract used by the user interface. |
| [`onClick`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/EmptyStateGuide.tsx#L21>) | callback contract | `onClick: () => void;` | Defines the on click contract used by the user interface. |
| [`onClick`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/EmptyStateGuide.tsx#L23>) | callback contract | `onClick: () => void;` | Defines the on click contract used by the user interface. |
| [`EmptyStateGuide`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/EmptyStateGuide.tsx#L44>) | React component | `export function EmptyStateGuide({ icon, title, description, steps, primaryAction, secondaryAction, actionSlot, py = 8, }: EmptyStateGuideProps)` | EmptyStateGuide — replaces the previous "No data, connect repos first" generic placeholder with something the user can actually act on. The shape is intentionally rigid: ONE icon, ONE title, ONE primary CTA, optionally a numbered step list. Pages that adopt this should put the primary action exactly where the user's eye lands first. Design rules (see feedback_ui_grounded_palette.md): - Icon background is slate-tinted at low opacity — not a saturated brand colour. Brand violet is reserved for the CTA button. - Steps are numbered with subtle bullets, not coloured pills. - Single semantic colour appears only inside the icon glyph (not its background) and even there, neutral by default. |

## `src-next/components/atoms/FeatureGate.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`FeatureGateProps`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/FeatureGate.tsx#L13>) | type | `export type FeatureGateProps = { /** Page id from 'internal/permission/capabilities.yaml.pages'. */ page: string /** Where to send the user when they don't have access. Defaults to * the org's dashboard. */ redirect?: string children: ReactNode }` | Defines the feature gate props contract used by the user interface. |
| [`FeatureGate`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/FeatureGate.tsx#L39>) | React component | `export function FeatureGate({ page, redirect, children }: FeatureGateProps)` | FeatureGate — wraps a route's element with a backend-driven page permission check. If the user's capabilities don't include the given 'page', the gate renders an explicit disabled-module state instead of auto-redirecting. While capabilities are still loading we show a small spinner instead of flashing the gated content. Usage in a route definition: { path: 'domains', element: <FeatureGate page="domains"><DomainsPage /></FeatureGate>, } Do NOT use FeatureGate to hide menu items — that's 'useCapabilities' inside the nav builder. This is for the route boundary so a user who types the URL directly still hits the gate. |
| [`FeatureLockedPreview`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/FeatureGate.tsx#L94>) | React component | `function FeatureLockedPreview({ page, state, paywall, onPrimary, }: { page: string state: PageAccess paywall?: Paywall onPrimary: () => void })` | Renders the feature locked preview interface for the user interface. |

## `src-next/components/atoms/SectionCard.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`SectionCardProps`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/SectionCard.tsx#L25>) | interface | `export interface SectionCardProps { title?: ReactNode /** Pre-rendered icon node, e.g. '<Plug size={16} />'. */ icon?: ReactNode /** Right-aligned header slot (status chip, button, count…). */ action?: ReactNode /** Inner padding (MUI spacing units). Default 2` | SectionCard — the one titled-panel primitive. Was previously: nearly every compound opened with the same boilerplate — an outlined Paper (divider border, ~2.5 padding) wrapping a header row of '<Icon/> + <Typography variant="subtitle1" fontWeight={700}>' and then the body. Same shell, copy-pasted per file, drifting in padding and gap. This is that shell, once: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ⬡ Title [action] │ ← header (optional) ├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ children │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 'action' is pushed to the right of the header (the common "status chip top-right" slot). Omit 'title' to get just the bordered card shell. |
| [`SectionCard`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/SectionCard.tsx#L37>) | React component | `export function SectionCard({ title, icon, action, padding = 2.5, children, sx, }: SectionCardProps)` | Renders the section card interface for the user interface. |

## `src-next/components/atoms/StatTile.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`StatTileOrientation`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/StatTile.tsx#L30>) | type | `export type StatTileOrientation = 'stacked' \| 'horizontal' \| 'centered'` | StatTile — the single metric-card primitive. Was previously: every domain hand-rolled its own labelled metric box (CloudPostureView 'Metric', ScoreTrendsView 'StatCard', ComplianceDashboardView 'StatCard', ScoreTrendPage 'StatCard', …) — same job, a different padding / value size / label style each time. One product, half a dozen metric-card dialects. This is the one true labelled-metric card. Three orientations cover every observed shape: stacked (default) horizontal centered ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ │ LABEL │ │ ⬡ 42 │ │ 42 │ │ 42 │ │ label sub │ │ label │ │ sub │ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘ NOT for: the exposure CSS-grid KPI row ('KpiTile'/'KpiRow' — its own visual system) or the compact "icon · label · value-right" status ROW in ArchOverview (that's a row, not a card). Use those where they already live; this atom is the standalone metric CARD. |
| [`StatTileProps`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/StatTile.tsx#L32>) | interface | `export interface StatTileProps { /** Caption. Rendered uppercase in 'stacked', natural-case otherwise. */ label: string /** Big number / text. Strings (e.g. "N/A", "C") render verbatim. */ value: number \| string /** Optional secondary line beneath the value/la` | Defines the stat tile props contract used by the user interface. |
| [`StatTile`](<../../src-next/components/atoms/StatTile.tsx#L57>) | React component | `export function StatTile({ label, value, sub, icon, color, orientation = 'stacked', valueSize = 28, loading = false, minWidth, grow = true, trailing, sx, }: StatTileProps)` | Renders the stat tile interface for the user interface. |

## `src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`IntelIssueRow`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L38>) | interface | `export interface IntelIssueRow { domain: string category: string severity: string description: string est_fix_time: string recommendation: string }` | Defines the intel issue row contract used by the user interface. |
| [`kernelFindingToIntelIssue`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L51>) | function | `export function kernelFindingToIntelIssue( domain: string, finding: ExternalFinding, ): IntelIssueRow` | Project one kernel-projected finding into the row shape DomainIntel renders. 'title_key' / 'desc_key' are i18n lookups; the fallback exposes the raw key when no translation exists rather than rendering an empty string. |
| [`kernelAssetsToIntelIssues`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L68>) | function | `export function kernelAssetsToIntelIssues( assets: KernelAsset[] \| undefined, ): IntelIssueRow[]` | Bulk variant — pulls every finding out of every asset, anchoring each to its parent canonical_value. Caller still does the sort (so the sort comparator can stay near the view's severity ordering). |
| [`kernelAssetsToDomainBuildings`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L81>) | function | `export function kernelAssetsToDomainBuildings( assets: KernelAsset[] \| undefined, ): CityBuilding[]` | Project each kernel external asset into the AssetCity3D building shape. Score / grade come straight from the kernel — no React-side rounding fallbacks beyond 'Math.round' for the display layer. |
| [`SLAIssueRow`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L97>) | interface | `export interface SLAIssueRow { domain: string category: string severity: string description: string }` | Defines the slaissue row contract used by the user interface. |
| [`SLAViolationRow`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L104>) | interface | `export interface SLAViolationRow extends SLAIssueRow { sla_hours: number overdue_by: string }` | Defines the slaviolation row contract used by the user interface. |
| [`externalIssuesToSLAIssues`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L113>) | function | `export function externalIssuesToSLAIssues( issues: OpenExternalIssue[] \| undefined, ): SLAIssueRow[]` | Open external issues come from '/external-issues' (the lighter shape that DOES include the CTEM lifecycle but doesn't carry SLA budget metadata). Used to populate the non-violated rows in the SLA monitor table. |
| [`ctemPrioritiesToSLAViolations`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L129>) | function | `export function ctemPrioritiesToSLAViolations( items: CTEMPriorityItem[] \| undefined, ): SLAViolationRow[]` | Breach state + SLA budget come from '/ctem-priorities' filtered to 'kind=external && breached'. 'overdue_by' is display-only — the underlying breach moment ('sla_breach_at') is backend-canonical, we just format the duration so the row reads as "Overdue 3d" instead of forcing the operator to read a timestamp. |
| [`ExternalThreatCounts`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L159>) | interface | `export interface ExternalThreatCounts { kev: number threatActor: number crownJewel: number }` | Counts that drive the dashboard's '<ExternalThreatStrip>' tiles: KEV-listed findings, findings naming a known threat actor, findings on a crown-jewel-tier asset. All three are flags the engine sets on the CTEM priority list; counting them locally is pure aggregation. Scope contract: this counts whatever the caller passes. The current call site (dashboard) hands over the full '/ctem-priorities' list which includes both 'external' and 'code' kinds — matching the pre-adapter behaviour. If a future caller needs external-only counts they should pre-filter the input or we add a dedicated variant; flipping the default here would silently change the dashboard's tile numbers. Returns zeros for undefined / empty input so the strip's "hide zero tiles" gate keeps working without per-tile defensive code. |
| [`externalThreatCountsFromCtem`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L165>) | function | `export function externalThreatCountsFromCtem( items: CTEMPriorityItem[] \| undefined, ): ExternalThreatCounts` | Implements external threat counts from ctem for the user interface. |
| [`SubdomainStats`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L178>) | interface | `export interface SubdomainStats { totalSubdomains: number resolvingSubdomains: number totalAssets: number }` | Defines the subdomain stats contract used by the user interface. |
| [`subdomainStats`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L194>) | function | `export function subdomainStats( assets: AttackSurfaceAsset[] \| undefined, ): SubdomainStats` | Count subdomains + resolving subdomains out of the raw attack-surface list. The 'resolves' flag lives in each asset's 'metadata' JSON blob, so this ends a 'JSON.parse(metadata)' that previously ran inside a React 'useMemo' (a "derive truth in component" smell, audit F2). This is a stop-gap: it still parses scanner-shaped metadata on the client. The real fix is backend shipping 'subdomain_count' / 'resolving_subdomain_count' on a posture-summary endpoint (audit BE gap 1) — at which point this helper is deleted, not relocated again. A malformed metadata blob counts as non-resolving (never throws). |
| [`PeerPercentileBand`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L211>) | interface | `export interface PeerPercentileBand { label: string tone: string }` | Defines the peer percentile band contract used by the user interface. |
| [`peerPercentileBand`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L226>) | function | `export function peerPercentileBand( score: number, latest: Record<number, { value: number }>, ): PeerPercentileBand \| null` | Map the org's avg score against the 5-point peer-baseline distribution (P25/P50/P75/P90/P95) and return a ranked-bucket label + tone. Bucketed, not interpolated: the corpus is small (n≈50–100) and operators read "Top 10%" as a rank, not a continuous statistic. Pure display arithmetic — the percentile VALUES are backend-canonical (peer-baseline snapshots); only the band labelling is display-layer, which is why it legitimately lives here and not on the server. Returns null when fewer than P50+P90 anchors exist (worker hasn't run yet) so the caller hides the chip rather than faking a band. |
| [`formatOverdue`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L255>) | function | `export function formatOverdue(slaBreachAt?: string): string` | "Overdue X" duration formatter. Backend-canonical breach moment comes in as RFC3339; the bucket boundaries ('<24h' shows hours, '≥24h' shows days) are display-layer choices and legitimately live in the frontend. Empty string for missing / un-parseable input so the caller renders nothing instead of "NaN d". |
| [`oldestSlaViolationDays`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalModel.ts#L270>) | function | `export function oldestSlaViolationDays( violations: SLAViolation[] \| undefined, ): number` | How many whole days ago the OLDEST currently-breached SLA was first detected. Drives the "X d" suffix on the dashboard hero SLA chip. Pure display arithmetic over the backend's 'detected_at' field — the truth that a row IS breached comes from the server. |

'score_available = // false' ⇒ avg_score / avg_display are null and avg_grade is // ` | Defines the external posture contract used by the user interface. |

Frontend resolves the i18n keys via tOr(); evidence carries scanner specifics (server header value, port number, file paths). |
| [`KernelAsset`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalPosture.ts#L143>) | interface | `export interface KernelAsset { resource_id: string type: 'subdomain' \| 'domain' \| 'ip' canonical_value: string display_name?: string sources: string[] score?: number grade?: string last_scanned?: string // RFC3339 confidence: number // 0..100 current_tier?: st` | One row of the kernel-backed external surface. Identity from kernel_resources; score/grade/last_scanned joined from score_state; findings projected from attack_surface metadata. |
| [`KernelExternalPosture`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalPosture.ts#L163>) | interface | `export interface KernelExternalPosture { org_id: string asset_count: number scored_count: number avg_score: number avg_grade: string assets: KernelAsset[] }` | Defines the kernel external posture contract used by the user interface. |
| [`getExternalPostureKernel`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalPosture.ts#L172>) | function | `export function getExternalPostureKernel(orgId: string): Promise<KernelExternalPosture>` | Retrieves external posture kernel for the user interface. |
| [`OpenExternalIssue`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalPosture.ts#L182>) | interface | `export interface OpenExternalIssue { id: string org_id: string domain: string category: string description: string severity: string fingerprint: string first_seen_at: string resolved_at?: string \| null mttr_hours?: number \| null }` | A single open issue from 'external_issue_tracker' — the CTEM Actions page reads these so that customers WITHOUT connected repos (i.e. empty 'code_alerts') still see "what's urgent" instead of an empty bench. Resolved rows are filtered server-side. |
| [`getOpenExternalIssues`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalPosture.ts#L195>) | function | `export function getOpenExternalIssues(orgId: string): Promise<{ org_id: string; count: number; issues: OpenExternalIssue[] }>` | Retrieves open external issues for the user interface. |
| [`extractHostFromAssetValue`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/_shared/externalPosture.ts#L219>) | function | `export function extractHostFromAssetValue(raw: string): string` | Extracts the bare hostname from any external asset's value or 'KernelAsset.canonical_value'. Both attack_surface scanners and the kernel projector emit a handful of shapes — without a single normalize step, 'Map<domain>' lookups across the two sources silently miss whenever scheme / case / suffix differs. Shapes handled: http_endpoint → "https://blog.flyto2.com" → "blog.flyto2.com" ssl_cert → "flyto2.com — TLS 1.3..." → "flyto2.com" waf → "flyto2.com — Cloudflare" → "flyto2.com" port_scan → "flyto2.com (1.2.3.4) ..." → "flyto2.com" breach_exposure → "blog.flyto2.com — 0..." → "blog.flyto2.com" dns_security → "flyto2.com" → "flyto2.com" subdomain → "blog.flyto2.com" → "blog.flyto2.com" kernel domain → "Example.COM" → "example.com" Falls back to the raw value (lowered, trimmed) when none of the delimiter shapes match. Empty string in → empty string out. |

| [`MomentumStrip`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx#L1013>) | React component | `function MomentumStrip({ delta, upgrades, downgrades, autofixReady, verified, onOpenAutofixQueue }: { delta: number upgrades: number downgrades: number autofixReady: number verified: number /** Open the fix queue drawer scoped to autofix-eligible items. * Auto` | MomentumStrip — 7-day trend signal as a four-stat row. Operators kept asking "are we getting better or worse?" — the gauge alone is a point-in-time snapshot, useless for that question. This strip pulls the answer to the top of the page: net score delta + up/down event counts + how much AutoFix / verification headroom we have to close the gap. |
| [`onOpenAutofixQueue`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx#L1022>) | callback contract | `onOpenAutofixQueue?: () => void` | Open the fix queue drawer scoped to autofix-eligible items. AutoFix is the only momentum-tile that's actionable — the other three are read-only trend signals. |
| [`MomentumTile`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx#L1071>) | React component | `function MomentumTile({ icon, tone, label, value, sub, onClick }: { icon: React.ReactNode tone: string label: string value: string sub?: string onClick?: () => void })` | Renders the momentum tile interface for the user interface. |
| [`onClick`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx#L1077>) | callback contract | `onClick?: () => void` | Defines the on click contract used by the user interface. |
| [`CrossDimTile`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx#L1149>) | React component | `function CrossDimTile({ crossDim, onOpenFixQueue }: { crossDim: { total: number; reachable: number; openPR: number; autofix: number; pentestVerified: number } /** Open the right-side fix queue drawer with the matching filter. */ onOpenFixQueue?: (filter: 'tain` | CrossDimTile — THE PRODUCT MOAT visualised. For every critical+high finding the engine has joined, show how many ALSO have the joined-from-other-dimension signal lit up. A Bitsight-only or Snyk-only competitor cannot render any of these cells — they don't have the join. This tile makes the thesis visible on the front page instead of being buried in Pulse rows. |
| [`onOpenFixQueue`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx#L1152>) | callback contract | `onOpenFixQueue?: (filter: 'taint' \| 'pr' \| 'autofix' \| 'pentest') => void` | Open the right-side fix queue drawer with the matching filter. |

The CISO-grade row: phishing infra, dark-web leaks, supply chain risk, KEV listings, crown-jewel exposure. Each tile deep-links into the relevant view. |

## `src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`Props`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx#L28>) | interface | `interface Props { rows: DomainRow[] onSelect: (domain: string) => void onDelete?: (domain: string) => void /** Start a scan for a footprint/kernel-origin row (one carrying a * resourceId). Renders a gated "Scan this" button when provided. */ onScan?: (row: Dom` | Defines the props contract used by the user interface. |
| [`Props.onSelect`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx#L30>) | callback contract | `onSelect: (domain: string) => void` | Defines the on select contract used by the user interface. |
| [`Props.onDelete`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx#L31>) | callback contract | `onDelete?: (domain: string) => void` | Defines the on delete contract used by the user interface. |
| [`Props.onScan`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx#L34>) | callback contract | `onScan?: (row: DomainRow) => void` | Start a scan for a footprint/kernel-origin row (one carrying a resourceId). Renders a gated "Scan this" button when provided. |
| [`DomainGroup`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx#L41>) | interface | `interface DomainGroup { /** User-defined group name (from the root project's name field) */ name: string /** The root project ID (for group-level operations) */ groupId: string \| null /** All domains in this group -- flat, equal peers */ members: DomainRow[] }` | Defines the domain group contract used by the user interface. |
| [`groupRows`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/domains/GroupedDomainList.tsx#L62>) | function | `export function groupRows(rows: DomainRow[]): DomainGroup[]` | Group domains by parent_id. All domains in a group are EQUAL peers -- there's no hierarchy or indentation. The group header uses the root project's user-defined name. Exported so DomainsView (and any future consumer that needs the "groups · domains" count chip / breakdown) reuses the exact same keying rules — in particular the 'byId.has(pid)' guard that turns dangling parent refs into their own group instead of a phantom merge bucket. Diverging copies silently disagree with the rendered list. |

## `src-next/components/compounds/footprint/scene/palette.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`ScenePalette`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/footprint/scene/palette.ts#L39>) | interface | `export interface ScenePalette { background: string fogColor: string fogNear: number fogFar: number edgeColor: string edgeHighlight: string starCount: number // 0 = hide ambientIntensity: number haloOpacityNeutral: number haloOpacitySeed: number labelBg: string` | Per-mode scene palette. Light mode needs softer everything — a blackish navy backdrop next to MUI's '#ffffff' surrounding shell reads as "the 3D viewport is broken". On light, we want a cool off-white that pairs with 'background.default' and lets the brand violet still pop without competing emissive halos. |

The output is the real artifact POSTed to /fusion/mappings (and dry-run'd first) — it must satisfy importmap.LoadCustom exactly, so this mirrors that schema: provider / version / source.kind / trust_tier / severity_scales / records[]. |

For '[$.findings[*].severity, $.findings[*].key]' → { recordsMatch: "$.findings[*]", fields: ["$.severity", "$.key"] }. Falls back to a top-level array ("$[*]") or whole-doc ("$") when there is no "[*]" segment in the observed paths. |

| [`nodeFromPhase`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/red-team/KillChainStepper.tsx#L70>) | function | `function nodeFromPhase(status: string \| undefined): NodeState \| null` | Map a single backend phase status onto a node state. 'skipped' is treated as terminal-done (the phase ran and produced nothing, not "still pending"); 'error' lights as done so a stalled phase doesn't read as never-started. |
| [`deriveStates`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/red-team/KillChainStepper.tsx#L86>) | function | `function deriveStates(i: KillChainInputs): Record<NodeDef['key'], NodeState>` | Derive each node's state. Precedence is strict: backend phaseStatus is the source of truth and wins FIRST. Scan-count / evidence heuristics are only a hint, consulted when the governing phase(s) are unknown (pending / absent). A scan count can never flip a backend-confirmed phase (P1-18). |

The engine emits 'none' for an un-graded cell (honest zero); the token table has no 'none' entry, so we route it to the neutral-empty tone. 'unclassified' rows are handled separately (never graded), so this never receives them. |

## `src-next/components/compounds/scanning/_shared.tsx`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`Loading`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/scanning/_shared.tsx#L31>) | React component | `export function Loading()` | Renders the loading interface for the user interface. |
| [`Empty`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/scanning/_shared.tsx#L35>) | React component | `export function Empty({ icon: Icon, text, description, action }: { icon: typeof LucideBox text: string description?: string action?: React.ReactNode })` | Renders the empty interface for the user interface. |
| [`ScanViewRoot`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/scanning/_shared.tsx#L58>) | React component | `export function ScanViewRoot({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode })` | Root container for all scan views. Pins its height to the parent flex slot and does NOT scroll — only child regions scroll. |
| [`ScanViewHeader`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/scanning/_shared.tsx#L88>) | React component | `export function ScanViewHeader({ title, subtitle, count }: { icon?: typeof LucideBox gradient?: string title: string subtitle: string count?: number countColor?: string })` | Page header used by scan views and several arch/domain views. Renders the Fuse typography hierarchy (text-3xl + text-md secondary) instead of the old gradient-icon-box. After the 2026-05-19 audit it turned out the gradient boxes were the single biggest visual-noise source on these pages — and the sidebar already carries section- level icon identity ('section.headerIcon'), so the per-page icon was duplicating that signal. 'icon' / 'gradient' / 'countColor' are kept in the signature so existing callers don't need a wholesale rewrite, but they are intentionally ignored. The optional 'count' still renders as a subtle chip aligned to the right of the title block. |
| [`SevBadge`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/scanning/_shared.tsx#L119>) | React component | `export function SevBadge({ severity }: { severity: string })` | Renders the sev badge interface for the user interface. |

Default 8 (dashboard card); pass higher (e.g. 50) * for the standalone page. */ limit?: number /** Compact (single-line per item, no grouping). Used inside dashboard * cards where vertical space is scarce.` | Defines the security news props contract used by the user interface. |
| [`SecurityNews`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/security/SecurityNews.tsx#L118>) | React component | `export function SecurityNews({ limit = 8, compact = false }: SecurityNewsProps = {})` | Renders the security news interface for the user interface. |
| [`NewsRow`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/security/SecurityNews.tsx#L206>) | React component | `function NewsRow({ item, compact }: { item: NewsItem; compact: boolean })` | Renders the news row interface for the user interface. |
| [`groupBy`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/security/SecurityNews.tsx#L349>) | function | `function groupBy<T, K extends string>(items: T[], keyFn: (item: T) => K): Partial<Record<K, T[]>>` | Implements group by for the user interface. |
| [`SecurityNewsView`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/security/SecurityNews.tsx#L380>) | React component | `export function SecurityNewsView()` | SecurityNewsView — page-level wrapper for the SecurityNews widget. Layout — two-region scroll per the workspace pattern: ┌────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Title + subtitle (fixed) │ flexShrink:0 ├────────────────────────────────────┤ │ News list (scrolls) │ flex:1 overflowY:auto │ ... │ └────────────────────────────────────┘ Outer Box uses 'overflow:hidden' and the inner list owns scroll — that way the header stays pinned while the operator scrolls through 50 items. Avoids the previous "header scrolls away with content" behaviour that operators flagged as breaking the war-room frame. No 'bgcolor' set anywhere — inherits the workspace neutral so the page never reads as "tinted blue/teal". |

## `src-next/components/compounds/threat-intel/countryCentroids.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`CountryCentroid`](<../../src-next/components/compounds/threat-intel/countryCentroids.ts#L12>) | interface | `export interface CountryCentroid { lat: number lng: number name: string }` | Country centroids — ISO-2 → {lat, lon, name}. Used by WorldHeatGlobe to position attacker dots + draw arcs from attacker country → org HQ. Coordinates from the google/dspl public-domain dataset; trimmed to the ~150 countries most likely to appear in threat-intel feeds. Missing entries fall through — the globe just doesn't draw an arc for that country (logged once during dev). |

Omitted = refresh all * (used by Sensor Map / IoC Lookup which span multiple feeds). */ source?: 'mitre' \| 'ransomware' \| 'sensors' \| 'all' /** Optional label override; defaults to the action verb. */ lab` | Defines the props contract used by the user interface. |

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |

| [`nodeEqualsType`](<../../src-next/components/tiptap/tiptap-extension/trailing-node-extension.ts#L5>) | function | `function nodeEqualsType({ types, node }: { types: NodeType \| NodeType[]; node: Node \| null })` | Implements node equals type for the user interface. |
| [`TrailingNodeOptions`](<../../src-next/components/tiptap/tiptap-extension/trailing-node-extension.ts#L15>) | interface | `export interface TrailingNodeOptions { node: string; notAfter: string[]; }` | Defines the trailing node options contract used by the user interface. |

| [`appendTransaction`](<../../src-next/components/tiptap/tiptap-extension/trailing-node-extension.ts#L39>) | object callback | `appendTransaction: (_, __, state) => { const { doc, tr, schema } = state; const shouldInsertNodeAtEnd = plugin.getState(state); const endPosition = doc.content.size; const type = schema.nodes[this.options.node]; if (!shouldInsertNodeAtEnd) { return null; } if ` | Implements append transaction for the user interface. |
| [`init`](<../../src-next/components/tiptap/tiptap-extension/trailing-node-extension.ts#L56>) | object callback | `init: (_, state) => { const lastNode = state.tr.doc.lastChild; return !nodeEqualsType({ node: lastNode, types: disabledNodes }); }` | Implements init for the user interface. |
| [`apply`](<../../src-next/components/tiptap/tiptap-extension/trailing-node-extension.ts#L61>) | object callback | `apply: (tr, value) => { if (!tr.docChanged) { return value; } const lastNode = tr.doc.lastChild; return !nodeEqualsType({ node: lastNode, types: disabledNodes }); }` | Implements apply for the user interface. |

Pass '() => true' (the default) to show everything — useful before the user has picked an org, or in tests / storybook. |
| [`getNavigationConfig`](<../../src-next/configs/navigationConfig.ts#L26>) | function | `export function getNavigationConfig(has?: FeatureCheck): FuseNavItemType[]` | Returns navigation config with i18n-resolved titles, filtered by the caller's capability snapshot. The 'has' predicate is typically 'caps.hasFeature' from useCapabilities; pass 'undefined' to skip feature filtering (shows everything). Tied to the engine's 'internal/permission/capabilities.yaml': changing which feature gates which nav id should be a YAML edit there + a one-line update to FEATURE_BY_ITEM above. |

Default 'all'. */ filter?: FixQueueFilter /** Optional pulse-item id to land on first. If absent, the drawer * starts on the highest-blast item that matches the filter` | Defines the fix queue open options contract used by the application. |

| [`canUseSurface`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L217>) | object callback | `canUseSurface: (surface: string) => { if (!ready) return false return surfaceAccess(surface).state === 'enabled' }` | Determines whether use surface for application state. |
| [`isSurfaceHidden`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L221>) | object callback | `isSurfaceHidden: (surface: string) => { if (!ready) return true return hiddenSurfaceSet.has(surface) \|\| surfaceAccess(surface).state === 'hidden' }` | Determines whether surface hidden for application state. |
| [`canSeePage`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L225>) | object callback | `canSeePage: (page: string) => { if (!ready) return false return pageAccess(page).state === 'enabled' }` | Determines whether see page for application state. |
| [`canOpenPage`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L229>) | object callback | `canOpenPage: (page: string) => { if (!ready) return false const state = pageAccess(page).state return state === 'enabled' \|\| state === 'locked_preview' }` | Determines whether open page for application state. |
| [`canDoAction`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L234>) | object callback | `canDoAction: (action: string) => (ready ? permSet.has(action) : false)` | Determines whether do action for application state. |
| [`actionAccess`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L235>) | object callback | `actionAccess: (action: string) => (ready ? actionAccess(action) : undefined)` | Implements action access for application state. |
| [`canUseAction`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L236>) | object callback | `canUseAction: (action: string) => { if (!ready) return false if (unsupportedActionSet.has(action)) return false const commercial = actionAccess(action) if (commercial) return commercial.state === 'allowed' return permSet.has(action) }` | Determines whether use action for application state. |
| [`isActionUnsupported`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L243>) | object callback | `isActionUnsupported: (action: string) => { if (!ready) return true return unsupportedActionSet.has(action) \|\| actionAccess(action)?.state === 'blocked' }` | Determines whether action unsupported for application state. |
| [`paywallFor`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L247>) | object callback | `paywallFor: (key?: string) => (ready && key && !billingPreview ? paywalls[key] : undefined)` | Implements paywall for for application state. |
| [`isEdition`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L248>) | object callback | `isEdition: (edition: string) => (ready ? c?.edition === edition : false)` | Determines whether edition for application state. |
| [`providerFor`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L249>) | object callback | `providerFor: (provider: keyof EditionProviders) => (ready ? c?.providers?.[provider] : undefined)` | Implements provider for for application state. |
| [`hasFeature`](<../../src-next/hooks/useCapabilities.ts#L250>) | object callback | `hasFeature: (feature: string) => (ready ? featureSet.has(feature) : false)` | Determines whether feature for application state. |

| [`useDiscoverySeed`](<../../src-next/hooks/useDiscoveryStatus.ts#L117>) | React hook | `export function useDiscoverySeed(orgId: string \| undefined)` | useDiscoverySeed — call ONCE near the top of any page that cares about scan state (Domains, Pentest, Dashboard). Hits /discoveries/active on mount + every 30s as a fallback for SSE drops. When the server says "nothing active", the local "scanning" set clears — operator stops seeing the stuck chip. |
| [`queryFn`](<../../src-next/hooks/useDiscoveryStatus.ts#L120>) | object callback | `queryFn: async () => { if (!orgId) return { active: [] as ActiveDiscoveryRow[] } return request<{ active: ActiveDiscoveryRow[]; count: number }>( 'GET', '/api/v1/code/orgs/${orgId}/discoveries/active', ) }` | Implements query fn for application state. |
| [`useCancelDiscovery`](<../../src-next/hooks/useDiscoveryStatus.ts#L143>) | React hook | `export function useCancelDiscovery(orgId: string \| undefined)` | useCancelDiscovery — mutation hook for the "✕ 取消" button. On success, clears the project from the local set immediately (don't wait for SSE) so the chip disappears. |
| [`mutationFn`](<../../src-next/hooks/useDiscoveryStatus.ts#L146>) | object callback | `mutationFn: async (projectId: string) => { if (!orgId) throw new Error('missing org id') return request<{ cancelled: boolean }>( 'POST', '/api/v1/code/orgs/${orgId}/discoveries/${projectId}/cancel', ) }` | Implements mutation fn for application state. |
| [`onSuccess`](<../../src-next/hooks/useDiscoveryStatus.ts#L152>) | object callback | `onSuccess: (_data, projectId) => { markDiscoveryComplete(projectId) // Re-fetch active so the rest of the page state stays consistent. qc.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: qk.exposure.discoveriesActive(orgId) }) }` | Handles success for application state. |

## `src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`RepoScore`](<../../src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts#L12>) | interface | `export interface RepoScore { /** A3: 'null' when this repo isn't scorable (default Map miss). * Consumers MUST check 'scorable' before reading; do NOT render * ''--'' or ''F'' as a substitute for "no data". */ grade: string \| null /** A3: 'null' when not scora` | Defines the repo score contract used by application state. |
| [`normaliseRepoScoreRow`](<../../src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts#L47>) | function | `export function normaliseRepoScoreRow(rs: RepoScoreResultServer): RepoScore` | normaliseRepoScoreRow — exported for tests. The wire's RepoScoreResultServer carries 'grade / raw / display' as non-nullable fields even when 'scorable === false' — backends sometimes ship default zeros (0 / "F" / 250) in those slots. That's exactly the "fake zero vs no-data" confusion the A3 envelope was designed to prevent. Scrub here so downstream consumers can rely on 'scorable === false ⇒ raw/display/grade all null' without per-callsite re-checks. Pure function — same input always yields same output. Tests call it directly so the contract is enforced at the source-of- truth layer, not just at the Map-miss boundary in getRepoScore. |
| [`useRepoScoresState`](<../../src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts#L62>) | React hook | `export function useRepoScoresState()` | Returns the score map plus readiness metadata. Use this when a caller needs to decide between loading/error/empty/data states; 'useRepoScores()' remains as the backwards-compatible display-only wrapper. |
| [`queryFn`](<../../src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts#L68>) | object callback | `queryFn: () => getComputedScore(org!.id)` | Implements query fn for application state. |
| [`useRepoScores`](<../../src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts#L93>) | React hook | `export function useRepoScores()` | Returns a Map<repo_id, RepoScore> from the unified computed-score engine. |
| [`getRepoScore`](<../../src-next/hooks/useRepoScores.ts#L106>) | function | `export function getRepoScore(scoreMap: Map<string, RepoScore>, repoId: string): RepoScore` | Get a single repo's unified score. Returns nullable fields when the repo has no scorable entry — consumers MUST branch on 'scorable' before reading 'raw / display / grade'. Defaulting the missing case to '{ grade: '--', raw: 0, display: 250, scorable: false }' (the pre-A3 behaviour) silently rendered unscored repos as the lowest-scored repo on the dashboard; see SCORING_CONTRACT A3 / API_RESPONSE_FIELD_MAP Critical #4. |

## `src-next/lib/cloud/authConfig.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`PentestAuthConfig`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/authConfig.ts#L24>) | type | `export type PentestAuthConfig = \| { kind: 'none' } \| { kind: 'cookie'; value: string } \| { kind: 'bearer'; value: string } \| { kind: 'headers'; headers: Record<string, string> } \| { kind: 'login' loginUrl: string method?: 'POST' \| 'GET' body?: Record<string, u` | Pentest authentication config — credentials / session material for authenticated scanning. Two entry shapes the user can set (stored in pentest.config.auth): 1. Static session (simplest, use for short-lived tests): { kind: 'cookie', value: 'sid=abc; token=xyz' } { kind: 'bearer', value: 'eyJhbGci...' } { kind: 'headers', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'xxx', 'X-Csrf': 'yyy' } } 2. Dynamic login (re-authenticates per campaign, robuster): { kind: 'login', loginUrl: 'https://.../login', method: 'POST', body: {...}, capture: 'cookie' \| 'bearer' \| 'header:X-Auth' } // runner executes this as a FIRST step and captures the // resulting credential; later rounds reference {{auth_*}}. Red team playbooks referencing '{{auth_cookie}}' / '{{auth_bearer}}' / '{{auth_header_<NAME>}}' in header values get the captured creds injected via params expansion. Playbooks without those templates remain unauthenticated (backwards compat). |
| [`readAuthConfig`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/authConfig.ts#L43>) | function | `export function readAuthConfig(rawConfig: string \| null \| undefined): PentestAuthConfig` | Parse the auth field from pentest.config JSON, tolerantly. |
| [`authToTemplateVars`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/authConfig.ts#L73>) | function | `export function authToTemplateVars(auth: PentestAuthConfig): Record<string, string>` | Convert a static auth config into flat template variables the playbook YAML can reference. Dynamic (login) configs are NOT handled here — they require a runtime login step (see generateLoginPrelude below). The template keys are: auth_cookie — full Cookie header value auth_bearer — just the token (without "Bearer " prefix) auth_bearer_hdr — "Bearer <token>" for Authorization header auth_header_<KEY> — custom header value, KEY uppercased |
| [`generateLoginPrelude`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/authConfig.ts#L106>) | function | `export function generateLoginPrelude(auth: PentestAuthConfig): string \| null` | Synthesize a one-step YAML playbook that logs in and captures the credential to the execution context. The executor then makes the capture available to subsequent rounds via params. Returns null when no dynamic login is needed. |

Defaults to false; the * /workflows/run endpoint accepts anonymous callers. */ requireAuth?: boolean }` | Defines the cloud request options contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |

The token is no longer placed in the query string (it would leak into server logs, Referer headers, and browser history). Instead callers should pass the token as the first message after 'onopen', or use the WebSocket subprotocol header via 'cloudWsConnect'. |
| [`cloudWsConnect`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/client.ts#L69>) | function | `export async function cloudWsConnect(path: string): Promise<{ url: string; token: string \| null }>` | Open a WebSocket that authenticates via the first binary message after 'onopen' instead of putting the token in the URL. The cloud worker expects an initial text frame 'AUTH <token>' before streaming data. Falls back to unauthenticated if no identity session is active. |

## `src-next/lib/cloud/eventTypes.gen.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`EngineEventType`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/eventTypes.gen.ts#L207>) | type | `export type EngineEventType = typeof ENGINE_EVENT_TYPES[number]` | Defines the engine event type contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`assertExhaustiveEvent`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/eventTypes.gen.ts#L227>) | function | `export function assertExhaustiveEvent(t: never): void` | Compile-time exhaustiveness check. Use in the default branch of a switch on event.type: switch (e.type as EngineEventType) { case 'scan.queued': … default: assertExhaustiveEvent(e.type as never) } The 'never' parameter type means a missing case is a TypeScript compile error — adding a new event server-side without handling it here breaks CI immediately. At runtime we warn instead of throwing so an engine that ships ahead of the frontend (e.g. a canary deploy emitting a brand-new event type) doesn't crash users' tabs. The compile-time bound is what catches the bug class; the runtime is intentionally lenient. |

## `src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`Phase`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L19>) | type | `export type Phase = \| 'baseline' // recon only; never sends payloads \| 'probe' // scanner-style, sends safe probes, ranks suspects \| 'verify' // focused payload per suspect, confirms with evidence \| 'recheck' // re-fire confirmed findings with payload + sessio` | Red-team campaign as a 5-phase state machine. Baseline → Probe → Verify → Recheck → Report Each phase has a FIXED-SCHEMA output ('intel: T'). The next phase ONLY sees that intel, never the raw round-by-round history. This bounds context to ~200-500 tokens per LLM call regardless of how much probing happened — eliminates the context-inflation hallucination mode the linear planner suffered from. Why not include a 6th "attack" phase: exploitation raises legal / trust / insurance issues the product doesn't want to carry today. Impact Simulation (proving a confirmed finding IS exploitable under controlled payloads but without data exfil) is the right v2 phase — recorded here for design continuity. |
| [`nextPhase`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L31>) | function | `export function nextPhase(p: Phase): Phase \| null` | Next phase, or null when the campaign is complete. |
| [`Evidence`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L40>) | interface | `export interface Evidence { url: string method: string status?: number timingMs?: number /** Short (≤300 char) response snippet that captures the signal. */ snippet?: string /** When the probe used a custom payload, it's recorded here so the * recheck phase ca` | A single observable datapoint that backs a suspect / finding. |
| [`BaselineIntel`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L57>) | interface | `export interface BaselineIntel { targetUrl: string /** Detected tech — user-facing framework names only, not versions. */ tech: string[] /** Endpoints discovered by passive recon (sitemap, robots, OPTIONS * responses, HEAD scans). No fuzzing here. */ endpoints` | Defines the baseline intel contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`Suspect`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L73>) | interface | `export interface Suspect { id: string class: string // category from flyto-ai's blueprint catalogue endpoint: string parameter?: string /** WHY we suspect — signals extracted from baseline + light probes. */ signal: string /** 0-1; high-confidence ones go stra` | Defines the suspect contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`ProbeIntel`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L84>) | interface | `export interface ProbeIntel { suspects: Suspect[] /** Categories we actively tested but saw nothing — lets recheck / * report honestly say "we checked A, B, C and they were clean". */ testedCategoriesClean: string[] }` | Defines the probe intel contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`ConfirmedFinding`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L91>) | interface | `export interface ConfirmedFinding { id: string class: string endpoint: string parameter?: string /** The payload that triggered the signal. */ proofPayload: string /** Server response snippet that proves the finding (e.g. SQL error). */ responseSnippet: string` | Defines the confirmed finding contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`VerifyIntel`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L106>) | interface | `export interface VerifyIntel { confirmed: ConfirmedFinding[] rejected: Array<{ suspectId: string reason: string }> }` | Defines the verify intel contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`ProvenFinding`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L114>) | interface | `export interface ProvenFinding extends ConfirmedFinding { /** Independent evidence across payload + session variance. */ repro: Evidence[] /** True when signal survives clearing cookies / Authorization headers. */ sessionIndependent: boolean /** True when sign` | Defines the proven finding contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`RecheckIntel`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L125>) | interface | `export interface RecheckIntel { proven: ProvenFinding[] flaky: Array<{ finding: ConfirmedFinding reason: string // "payload-specific" / "session-dependent" / "timing-flaky" }> }` | Defines the recheck intel contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`ReportIntel`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L133>) | interface | `export interface ReportIntel { priorities: Array<{ findingId: string class: string endpoint: string rationale: string // one-line "why it matters" businessImpact: string // plain-English consequence fixBucket: 'this_week' \| 'this_sprint' \| 'backlog' chainHint?` | Defines the report intel contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`IntelFor`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L156>) | type | `export type IntelFor<P extends Phase> = P extends 'baseline' ? BaselineIntel : P extends 'probe' ? ProbeIntel : P extends 'verify' ? VerifyIntel : P extends 'recheck' ? RecheckIntel : P extends 'report' ? ReportIntel : never` | Defines the intel for contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`PhaseResult`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/phases.ts#L164>) | interface | `export interface PhaseResult<P extends Phase = Phase> { phase: P /** 1-2 sentences, rendered verbatim in the UI's phase timeline. */ summary: string intel: IntelFor<P> /** Raw evidence captured during the phase — persisted per-phase, * NOT fed into the next ph` | Defines the phase result contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |

## `src-next/lib/cloud/pipelineEvents.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`PipelineEventType`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/pipelineEvents.ts#L21>) | type | `export type PipelineEventType = \| 'pipeline_run.created' \| 'pipeline_run.phase' \| 'pipeline_run.evidence' \| 'pipeline_run.finalized'` | Tiny module-level pub/sub for pipeline SSE events. Why exists: useOrgEvents is mounted once at WorkspacePage root and pushes updates into React Query via invalidateQueries. The pipeline hook (useCampaignPipeline) doesn't yet use React Query (it has its own imperative state machine), so the SSE bridge needs a different conduit. This file is that conduit. Once the orchestrator moves engine-side (Phase C) and the hook becomes a thin observer over React Query, this can collapse into a normal queryClient.setQueryData() flow and we'll delete the file. Why not window CustomEvent: same-origin window events propagate across iframes and could confuse tooling (Sentry, devtools). A plain ref-counted listener set is enough for in-process delivery and adds no global mutation. |
| [`PipelineEvent`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/pipelineEvents.ts#L27>) | interface | `export interface PipelineEvent { type: PipelineEventType /** Org id the event belongs to. Listeners filter on this. */ orgId: string /** Decoded payload from the SSE frame. Shape depends on type. */ payload: Record<string, unknown> }` | Defines the pipeline event contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`Listener`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/pipelineEvents.ts#L35>) | type | `type Listener = (e: PipelineEvent) => void` | Defines the listener contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`subscribePipelineEvents`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/pipelineEvents.ts#L40>) | function | `export function subscribePipelineEvents(fn: Listener): () => void` | Subscribe; returns an unsubscribe function. Safe to call from React effects — the cleanup return mirrors the pattern. |
| [`emitPipelineEvent`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/pipelineEvents.ts#L47>) | function | `export function emitPipelineEvent(e: PipelineEvent): void` | Emit synchronously to all subscribers. Errors in one listener must not stop the rest — wrap each in try/catch. |

The campaign` | Defines the seed playbook contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |

Used by the campaign orchestrator so we don't fire YAMLs that refer to variables we never populated (e.g. auth_token on an unauthenticated target would leave '{{auth_token}}' literal in the request). |

## `src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`CampaignScope`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L14>) | interface | `export interface CampaignScope { /** Hosts that may be contacted. Supports a single leading-wildcard: * "api.example.com" → exact match only * "*.example.com" → matches example.com + any subdomain * Empty / undefined list = nothing allowed (hard block). */ all` | Scope Guard — enforces "what's in range for this campaign" before any YAML leaves the client. Parses every URL out of the YAML (after template-var expansion), matches host + path + method against the declared scope, and returns violations. The hook aborts a round when violations come back. We intentionally run this client-side because the AI-generated YAMLs and the seed library can both reference places we never intended to touch — Cloud worker may accept anything, so this is the operator's red-line. |
| [`ScopeViolation`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L30>) | interface | `export interface ScopeViolation { step: string reason: string url?: string method?: string }` | Defines the scope violation contract used by the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`isHostAllowed`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L38>) | function | `export function isHostAllowed(host: string, allowed: string[]): boolean` | Match host against "*.example.com" / "example.com" / exact. |
| [`isPathAllowed`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L53>) | function | `function isPathAllowed(path: string, prefixes: string[] \| undefined): boolean` | Determines whether path allowed for the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`isMethodAllowed`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L59>) | function | `function isMethodAllowed(method: string, methods: string[] \| undefined): boolean` | Determines whether method allowed for the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`expandTemplate`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L67>) | function | `export function expandTemplate(s: string, params: Record<string, string>): string` | Expand '{{var}}' tokens with the provided param map. Unresolved tokens stay in place so downstream code can flag them. |
| [`extractRequests`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L82>) | function | `export function extractRequests(yaml: string): Array<{ url: string; method: string; step: string }>` | Pull (url, method) pairs out of a flyto-core pentest YAML. The canonical schema uses: - module: http.request → params.url + params.method - module: http.batch → params.requests: [{ method, url }, ...] - module: browser.goto → params.url (method implicit GET) This parser is regex-based because the YAML may contain embedded JS blocks (browser.evaluate) that a strict YAML parser would choke on — and we only care about the URL payload, not full AST fidelity. |
| [`validateYamlInScope`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L140>) | function | `export function validateYamlInScope( yaml: string, scope: CampaignScope, params: Record<string, string> = {}, ): ScopeViolation[]` | Validates yaml in scope for the Flyto2 Cloud client. |
| [`scopeFromTargetUrl`](<../../src-next/lib/cloud/scope.ts#L185>) | function | `export function scopeFromTargetUrl(targetUrl: string, extraHosts: string[] = []): CampaignScope` | Build a default scope that locks the campaign to the target URL's host. |

Contract: 'assetOrResourceId' carries a kernel 'resource_id' preferred — the backend resolves GetKernelResource first, then falls back to a legacy 'attack_surface.id' lookup. Callers (e.g. AssetMapView) pass 'node.resource_id'. The path segment name 'attack-surface/{id}' is historical; either id shape works. |

Contract: 'assetOrResourceId' carries a kernel 'resource_id' preferred — the backend resolves GetKernelResource first, then falls back to a legacy 'attack_surface.id' lookup. Callers (e.g. AssetMapView) pass 'node.resource_id'. The path segment name 'attack-surface/{id}' is historical; either id shape works. |

## `src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`SecurityIssue`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L6>) | interface | `export interface SecurityIssue { id: string type: string // "cve" \| "secret" \| "security_finding" severity: string // "CRITICAL" \| "HIGH" \| "MODERATE" \| "LOW" title: string description: string // Dedup key used by verify + status-change flows. fingerprint: str` | Defines the security issue contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`IssuesResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L41>) | interface | `export interface IssuesResponse { issues: SecurityIssue[] counts: { open: number; snoozed: number; ignored: number; solved: number; total: number } }` | Defines the issues response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`EnrichedSecurityIssue`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L53>) | interface | `export interface EnrichedSecurityIssue extends SecurityIssue { blast_radius?: number open_prs_touching?: PRRef[] taint_adjacency?: TaintRef \| null autofix_eligible?: boolean pentest_verdict?: PentestRef \| null }` | EnrichedSecurityIssue — what /issues?enrich=true returns. Embeds the original SecurityIssue plus correlate.LocationContext: open PRs, taint adjacency, AutoFix readiness, pentest verdict, and a computed blast_radius. Every context field is optional; missing means "we don't know", not "false". |
| [`EnrichedIssuesResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L61>) | interface | `export interface EnrichedIssuesResponse { issues: EnrichedSecurityIssue[] counts: { open: number; snoozed: number; ignored: number; solved: number; total: number } }` | Defines the enriched issues response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`IssueFilters`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L78>) | interface | `export interface IssueFilters { status?: string severity?: string type?: string repo?: string }` | IssueFilters — the server-side filters the /issues endpoint accepts (status / severity / type / repo). All four are aggregated client-side by the engine BEFORE the response is built, so a 5k-issue org never ships the full set down just to render 30 rows. The status 'counts' in the response are computed over ALL issues regardless of these filters (see handlers_issues.go#applyIssueFilters), so the UI's tab badges stay stable even when severity/type/repo narrow the table. 'repo' is sent as the canonical 'repo_id' query param; the engine still accepts the legacy '?repo=' alias for older clients. |
| [`appendIssueFilters`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L85>) | function | `function appendIssueFilters(qs: URLSearchParams, params?: IssueFilters)` | Implements append issue filters for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`getOrgIssues`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L93>) | function | `export function getOrgIssues(orgId: string, params?: IssueFilters)` | Retrieves org issues for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`getEnrichedOrgIssues`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L101>) | function | `export function getEnrichedOrgIssues(orgId: string, params?: IssueFilters)` | Same as getOrgIssues but with cross-dim correlate context. |
| [`updateIssueStatus`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L110>) | function | `export function updateIssueStatus(orgId: string, fingerprint: string, status: 'open' \| 'snoozed' \| 'ignored' \| 'solved', snoozeDays?: number)` | Updates issue status for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`CodeAlert`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L120>) | interface | `export interface CodeAlert { id: string repoId: string orgId?: string scanId?: string \| null category: string severity: string title: string description?: string filePath?: string \| null lineNumber?: number \| null fingerprint?: string status: string createdAt:` | Defines the code alert contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`PRRef`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L138>) | interface | `export interface PRRef { number: number title?: string url?: string head_branch?: string is_draft?: boolean opened_at?: string }` | Defines the prref contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`TaintRef`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L147>) | interface | `export interface TaintRef { categories?: string[] unsanitized_count?: number }` | Defines the taint ref contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`PentestRef`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L152>) | interface | `export interface PentestRef { project_id: string target_url?: string last_scan_at?: string critical_count?: number }` | Defines the pentest ref contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`EnrichedAlert`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L166>) | type | `export type EnrichedAlert = CodeAlert & { blast_radius: number open_prs_touching?: PRRef[] taint_adjacency?: TaintRef \| null autofix_eligible?: boolean pentest_verdict?: PentestRef \| null last_seen?: string }` | EnrichedAlert is what /alerts?enrich=true and /pulse return — a CodeAlert with cross-dimension context (open PRs touching the file, taint adjacency, AutoFix eligibility, pentest verdict, blast radius score). Every context field is optional; missing means "we don't know", not "false". |
| [`AlertListResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L175>) | interface | `export interface AlertListResponse { alerts: CodeAlert[] count: number }` | Defines the alert list response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`EnrichedAlertListResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L180>) | interface | `export interface EnrichedAlertListResponse { alerts: EnrichedAlert[] count: number }` | Defines the enriched alert list response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`listAlerts`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L185>) | function | `export function listAlerts(orgId: string, status?: string)` | Retrieves alerts for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`listEnrichedAlerts`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L191>) | function | `export function listEnrichedAlerts(orgId: string, status?: string)` | Same path, but with cross-dim context attached to each row. |
| [`PulseItem`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L206>) | interface | `export interface PulseItem { id: string // The engine emits 11 pulse sources (internal/correlate knownPulseSources). // Keep this union in lockstep so SOURCE_META renders each honestly instead of // collapsing cspm/identity/leak/divergence into the 'alert' (SA` | PulseItem — unified shape returned by /orgs/{id}/pulse. Same schema for every source (code alert / container / IaC / license / DAST / pentest); the 'source' discriminator drives icon + colour in <PulseRow>. Cross-dim context fields mirror EnrichedAlert. |
| [`PulseResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L237>) | interface | `export interface PulseResponse { items: PulseItem[] count: number // Dimension-awareness (engine convergence 2026-06-10): which known pulse // sources produced >=1 item this request vs produced zero. "missing" means // produced-zero-rows this request — NOT "un` | Defines the pulse response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`getOrgPulse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/issues.ts#L258>) | function | `export function getOrgPulse(orgId: string, since: string = '', limit: number = 50)` | Pulse — cross-dim "what should I look at right now" feed. Returns the unified PulseItem stream sorted by blast radius desc. Default 'since=""' (no time floor) so the user always sees their open backlog on first paint — narrowing comes from the UI's window picker, not the API default. |

Optional in the wire format (omitempty * on the Go side) so older rows discovered before the column * existed don't break parsing — but populated for every asset * produced by the cur` | Defines the attack surface asset contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`listPentestProjects`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L78>) | function | `export function listPentestProjects(orgId: string)` | Retrieves pentest projects for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`deletePentestProject`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L82>) | function | `export function deletePentestProject(projectId: string)` | Removes or resets pentest project for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`parseLinkedRepoId`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L93>) | function | `export function parseLinkedRepoId(config: string \| null \| undefined): string \| null` | P1-14: the BE auto-links a pentest project to a connected repo at create time (homepage ↔ target-host match) and stamps 'linked_repo_id' inside the project's 'config' JSON blob (the same blob handleLinkRepo / collectSASTContext read — no dedicated top-level column). Pull it out so the project row can surface a "linked → <repo>" chip. Returns null when no link exists (config empty / malformed / no match) — callers must NOT fabricate a link in that case. |
| [`deleteDomain`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L103>) | function | `export function deleteDomain(orgId: string, domain: string)` | Removes or resets domain for the Flyto2 Engine client. |

| [`PentestFinding`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L113>) | interface | `export interface PentestFinding { id: string scan_id: string project_id: string category: string severity: string title: string description: string evidence: string remediation: string status: string created_at: string }` | One persisted DAST finding — the detail behind a scan's aggregate counts. Lives in the pentest domain (pentest_findings), surfaced per-scan. |
| [`listPentestScanFindings`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L130>) | function | `export function listPentestScanFindings(projectId: string, scanId: string)` | Per-scan finding drill-down. The counts on PentestScan are the rollup; this returns the individual findings (header/TLS/port/API issues) behind them, from GET /pentests/{id}/scans/{scanId}/findings. |
| [`PentestFindingStatus`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L137>) | type | `export type PentestFindingStatus = 'open' \| 'triaged' \| 'resolved' \| 'false_positive'` | Defines the pentest finding status contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`updatePentestFindingStatus`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L141>) | function | `export function updatePentestFindingStatus( orgId: string, projectId: string, findingId: string, status: PentestFindingStatus, )` | Persist per-finding DAST triage so operator decisions survive rescans and feed the backend score recompute path. |
| [`triggerDiscovery`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L155>) | function | `export function triggerDiscovery(projectId: string)` | Implements trigger discovery for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`RunPentestResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L169>) | interface | `export interface RunPentestResponse { scan_id?: string project_id?: string status?: string scan_type?: string reused?: boolean error?: string retryable?: boolean /** P2-22: '/run' is the ACTIVE-traffic path — scan_mode:"active" / * emits_traffic:true — and is ` | Trigger a DAST pentest scan against a previously-created project. Engine handles concurrency (one in-flight scan per project) and reuses an existing queued/running scan instead of spawning a duplicate when the same project is hit twice in quick succession. Response shapes: - 201 { scan_id, status: "running" } — fresh scan kicked off - 200 { scan_id, status, reused: true } — already in-flight - 409 { error, retryable: true } — claim contention |
| [`runPentestScan`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L185>) | function | `export function runPentestScan(projectId: string)` | Implements run pentest scan for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`ScanAssetType`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L194>) | type | `export type ScanAssetType = \| 'ssl_cert' \| 'tech_stack' \| 'waf' \| 'whois' \| 'dns_security' \| 'api_docs' \| 'graphql' \| 'error_fp' \| 'pagespeed' \| 'port_scan' \| 'api_verify' \| 'js_bundle' \| 'sensitive_files'` | Asset types accepted by /scan-asset. Mirrors the engine's switch — adding a new scanner there means adding it here too. |
| [`scanAsset`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L205>) | function | `export function scanAsset(projectId: string, domain: string, assetType: ScanAssetType)` | Trigger ONE scanner against ONE (sub)domain. Used by the war room's "Run scan" button on empty tabs that the auto-cascade didn't cover (PageSpeed / port_scan / api_verify only run on the root by default). Returns immediately — SSE delivers the result. |
| [`discoverAllDomains`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L227>) | function | `export function discoverAllDomains(orgId: string)` | '/discover-all' response. 'triggered' is the legacy project-scan count (back-compat); 'scanning' is the real total including project-less kernel/footprint domains, and 'kernel_domains' is how many of those fired. A footprint-only org has triggered=0 but scanning>0 — read 'scanning' (falling back to the sum) for the user-facing count, or footprint scans look like a no-op. |
| [`AttackSurfacePageSpeedAvg`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L233>) | interface | `export interface AttackSurfacePageSpeedAvg { performance: number accessibility: number best_practices: number seo: number domain_count: number }` | Defines the attack surface page speed avg contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |

| [`AttackSurfaceDomain`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L246>) | interface | `export interface AttackSurfaceDomain { value: string asset_type?: string status?: string review_status?: string validation_status?: string confidence?: number tier?: string }` | Defines the attack surface domain contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |

| [`listAttackSurface`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L267>) | function | `export function listAttackSurface(orgId: string, includeCandidates = false)` | Retrieves attack surface for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`BrandProtectionEvidence`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/pentest.ts#L283>) | interface | `export interface BrandProtectionEvidence { type: string label: string value?: string axis?: 'relationship' \| 'intent' \| 'ownership' \| 'provider' \| string severity?: string provider?: string description?: string score?: number reference_id?: string }` | Brand Protection (impersonation) response. Read from the dedicated /brand-protection endpoint, NOT the kernel-backed /attack-surface reader (which drops non-infrastructure asset types like impersonation). Each asset carries a WHOIS 'ownership' verdict in its metadata: - third_party : a real impersonator (different registrant than your domain) - self_owned : your OWN defensive registration (same registrant) — not a threat - unknown : registrant redacted/privacy-protected — can't attribute |
