| [`parseJsonArray`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/threatIntel.ts#L322>) | function | `export function parseJsonArray(s?: string): string[]` | Helper for the JSON-array string columns (aliases, sectors, platforms, techniques). Returns [] on any parse failure so rendering never crashes on a malformed row. TODO(backend-truth, m1/m2): the engine stores these as JSON strings and ships them raw — the frontend then has to JSON.parse every row for display + .length counts. Schema should return typed arrays ('aliases: string[]', 'techniques: string[]') plus pre-computed counts ('technique_count') so the parser becomes dead code. See FRONTEND_LOGIC_AUDIT_2026_05_24.md#m1 |

## `src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`TimelineLayer`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L37>) | type | `export type TimelineLayer = 'L1' \| 'L2' \| 'L3' \| 'L4'` | Defines the timeline layer contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`TimelineKind`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L41>) | type | `export type TimelineKind = \| 'scan' \| 'pentest' \| 'score' \| 'alert' \| 'asset' \| 'confidence' \| 'path_status' \| 'decision'` | L1 raw-event kinds (scan/pentest/score/alert/asset) + the per-layer synthetic kinds the engine emits for L2/L3/L4 rows. |
| [`TimelineProvenance`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L52>) | interface | `export interface TimelineProvenance { source: string source_type: string }` | Provenance of an L2/L3 signal — where the confidence/verdict came from. |
| [`TimelineItemPayload`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L63>) | interface | `export interface TimelineItemPayload { confidence?: number provenance?: TimelineProvenance verified?: boolean verified_method?: string /** Layers thread arbitrary extra fields; keep them addressable without 'any'. */ [k: string]: unknown }` | Optional per-item payload. L2/L3 items carry confidence + provenance + verified/verified_method; L4 (operator decision) carries NONE of these (an operator action is not evidence). Everything is optional so a single 'TimelineItem' shape covers all four layers — branch on 'layer'/'kind'. |
| [`TimelineItem`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L80>) | interface | `export interface TimelineItem { id: string layer: TimelineLayer kind: TimelineKind /** RFC3339 timestamp the event was observed at. */ ts: string title?: string summary?: string /** Path id (L3) or finding fingerprint (L2/L4) this row belongs to. */ subject_id` | One row in the layered audit timeline. Aligns the engine 'FeedItem' shape (kind ∈ scan\|pentest\|score\|alert\|asset (L1) \| confidence (L2) \| path_status (L3) \| decision (L4)) with the optional evidence payload. NOTE: named 'TimelineItem' (NOT 'FeedItem') to avoid colliding with the existing 'history/history-feed' 'FeedItem' re-exported from index.ts. |
| [`OrgTimeline`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L95>) | interface | `export interface OrgTimeline { org_id: string from: string to: string layers: TimelineLayer[] subject_id: string items: TimelineItem[] count: number }` | GET /orgs/{id}/timeline response envelope. |
| [`GetOrgTimelineOptions`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L106>) | interface | `export interface GetOrgTimelineOptions { /** Relative window (e.g. "24h", "7d"). Mutually exclusive with from/to. */ since?: string /** Absolute window start (RFC3339). */ from?: string /** Absolute window end (RFC3339). */ to?: string /** Restrict to specific` | Options for 'getOrgTimeline'. 'layers' is serialized as 'L1,L2,...'. |
| [`getOrgTimeline`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L119>) | function | `export function getOrgTimeline( orgId: string, opts: GetOrgTimelineOptions = {}, ): Promise<OrgTimeline>` | Retrieves org timeline for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`PathStatusEventType`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L138>) | type | `export type PathStatusEventType = \| 'path.created' \| 'status_changed' \| 'finding_added' \| 'finding_removed' \| 'pentest_confirmed' \| 'regression_detected'` | Defines the path status event type contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`PathStatusEvent`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L146>) | interface | `export interface PathStatusEvent { attack_path_id: string from_status: string to_status: string event_type: PathStatusEventType actor: string reason: string source: string verified: boolean verified_method: string /** Chain probability captured at the moment o` | Defines the path status event contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`PathHistory`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L162>) | interface | `export interface PathHistory { path_id: string org_id: string events: PathStatusEvent[] count: number }` | GET /paths/{id}/history response envelope. |
| [`getPathHistory`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/timeline.ts#L173>) | function | `export function getPathHistory(orgId: string, pathId: string): Promise<PathHistory>` | NOTE the route shape: the path id is in the URL but 'org_id' is a REQUIRED query param (not a path segment). |

## `src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`VerifyFindingResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L7>) | interface | `export interface VerifyFindingResponse { execution_id: string live_view_url?: string yaml?: string status: 'queued' \| 'running' \| 'passed' \| 'failed' \| 'error' /** Static mode returns an immediate verdict — 'reachable' for now. * Dynamic mode returns the termi` | Defines the verify finding response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerdictEvidence`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L42>) | interface | `export interface VerdictEvidence { /** Upstream-source confidence for this CVE, 0..1. */ CVEMetaConfidence: number /** Layer 1 saw the package imported. */ L1Imported: boolean /** cvemeta has function-level metadata for this CVE. */ L2HasVulnFunctions: boolean` | Static-verdict evidence signals, mirroring the engine's 'verdictEvidence' struct (api/verify_verdict.go). That struct has NO json tags and is emitted via '"evidence": v.Evidence', so Go's encoding/json defaults to the exact Go field names — hence the capitalized keys here (verified against the backend wire shape). CVEMetaConfidence is a 0..1 float (0.85 GHSA-derived, 0.45 AI fallback, 0 when no metadata). |
| [`VerifyMode`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L59>) | type | `export type VerifyMode = 'static' \| 'dynamic'` | Defines the verify mode contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerifyVerdict`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L68>) | type | `export type VerifyVerdict = \| 'exploitable' // dynamic probe + CVE-specific payload matched (future) \| 'suspected_exploitable' // dynamic probe observed no input-validation — pattern match only \| 'sanitized' \| 'likely_sanitized' // dynamic probe observed block` | Verdict vocabulary — hedged wherever we can't prove a definitive answer. 'suspected_exploitable' / 'likely_sanitized' were introduced 2026-04-23 after Chester pushed back on bare "exploitable" readings from generic-payload probes: those are pattern matches, not proofs, and the UI must not claim more than it observed. Keep the hedged variants and plain ones both accepted — older rows still carry the plain forms. |
| [`VerifyConfidence`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L77>) | type | `export type VerifyConfidence = 'high' \| 'medium' \| 'low'` | Defines the verify confidence contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`WorkflowExecution`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L79>) | interface | `export interface WorkflowExecution { id: string orgId: string repoId?: string findingFp: string executionId: string status: 'queued' \| 'running' \| 'passed' \| 'failed' \| 'error' verdict?: VerifyVerdict yaml: string liveViewUrl?: string evidenceUrl?: string erro` | Defines the workflow execution contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerifyRequestOptions`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L95>) | interface | `export interface VerifyRequestOptions { mode: VerifyMode /** Required only in dynamic mode. */ targetUrl?: string /** Required for dynamic mode on a cloud deployment — the UI must * gather explicit user consent because dynamic probing sends * synthetic attack ` | Defines the verify request options contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`verifyFinding`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L106>) | function | `export function verifyFinding( repoId: string, fingerprint: string, opts: VerifyRequestOptions, )` | Validates finding for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`getWorkflowExecution`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L122>) | function | `export function getWorkflowExecution(executionId: string)` | Retrieves workflow execution for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerifyTargets`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L137>) | interface | `export interface VerifyTargets { targets: string[] }` | Defines the verify targets contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`getVerifyTargets`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L141>) | function | `export function getVerifyTargets(repoId: string)` | Retrieves verify targets for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`updateVerifyTargets`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L148>) | function | `export function updateVerifyTargets(repoId: string, targets: string[])` | Updates verify targets for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerifyExplainResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L163>) | interface | `export interface VerifyExplainResponse { explanation: string cached: boolean }` | Defines the verify explain response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`explainExecution`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/code/verify.ts#L168>) | function | `export function explainExecution(executionId: string)` | Implements explain execution for the Flyto2 Engine client. |

| [`AssetEvidenceResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/assetEvidence.ts#L94>) | interface | `export interface AssetEvidenceResponse { org_id: string asset_type: string asset_key: string /** null when the asset has never been observed (UI: "not scanned yet"). */ state: AssetState \| null /** Chronological oldest-first. */ observations: RawObservation[] ` | Defines the asset evidence response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`listAssetStates`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/assetEvidence.ts#L113>) | function | `export function listAssetStates( orgId: string, opts: { assetType?: string; status?: AssetStateStatus; includePending?: boolean } = {}, )` | List asset_states for one org, optionally narrowed by type / status. Default response excludes 'pending' (transient, no UI value). |
| [`getAssetEvidence`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/assetEvidence.ts#L133>) | function | `export function getAssetEvidence(orgId: string, assetType: string, assetKey: string)` | Full evidence bundle for one (asset_type, asset_key). Returns state=null when never observed — caller renders "not scanned yet" instead of treating it as an error. |
| [`downloadAuditExport`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/assetEvidence.ts#L151>) | function | `export async function downloadAuditExport( orgId: string, assetType: string, opts: { status?: AssetStateStatus } = {}, ): Promise<{ filename: string; bytes: number; bundleHash: string }>` | Compliance audit-export endpoint. Returns a JSON Blob the caller triggers as a browser download. Backend stamps the response with Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=flyto-audit-<org>-<type>.json, but we still synthesize the filename client-side because the fetch path doesn't expose response headers conveniently. Status filter mirrors the engine: empty = all assets, 'refuted' = only ones the engine has rejected, etc. |

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`AssetTier`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L10>) | type | `export type AssetTier = 'crown_jewel' \| 'customer_facing' \| 'internal' \| 'sandbox'` | Defines the asset tier contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerificationState`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L12>) | type | `export type VerificationState = \| 'unverified' \| 'pending_verify' \| 'verified_fixed' \| 'reopened' \| 'false_positive'` | Defines the verification state contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`VerificationMethod`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L19>) | type | `export type VerificationMethod = \| 'passive' \| 'active_verified' \| 'authenticated_verified' \| 'manual_confirmed'` | Defines the verification method contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`ImpactEstimate`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L25>) | interface | `export interface ImpactEstimate { low_usd: number mid_usd: number high_usd: number confidence: 'low' \| 'medium' \| 'high' label: string // "Potential financial exposure" methodology: string input_summary: string[] benchmark_source: string }` | Defines the impact estimate contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`CTEMPriorityItem`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L36>) | interface | `export interface CTEMPriorityItem { kind: 'external' \| 'code' id: string fingerprint: string title: string description: string severity: string effective_severity: string priority_score: number category: string domain?: string repo_id?: string asset_tier: Asse` | Defines the ctempriority item contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`PriorityBreakdown`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L130>) | interface | `export interface PriorityBreakdown { base_severity_weight: number // 60 critical / 40 high / 20 medium / 5 low tier_multiplier: number // 1.5 / 1.2 / 1.0 / 0.5 tier_contribution: number // base * (tier - 1) exploit_multiplier: number // 1.0 = no signal exploit` | Per-component decomposition of priority_score. See CTEMPriorityItem.priority_breakdown for the visual contract. Sum identity (modulo per-component int rounding): base + tier_contribution + exploit_contribution + mitigation_contribution + clamp_contribution === priority_score clamp_contribution captures the 0-100 cap on priority_score so the additive sum never over-shoots the final score (a high finding like critical+crown_jewel+KEV pre-clamps at 135 but final is 100 → clamp_contribution = -35). Frontend renders this segment as a visual cap so the bar doesn't draw past 100%. |
| [`CTEMPriorityResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L142>) | interface | `export interface CTEMPriorityResponse { org_id: string count: number items: CTEMPriorityItem[] /** Echo of the canonical sort key the server applied. Always * populated — empty / unknown '?sort=' values resolve to * "priority" server-side, so the echo carries ` | Defines the ctempriority response contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`CTEMSortKey`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L167>) | type | `export type CTEMSortKey = 'priority' \| 'sla' \| 'severity' \| 'first_seen' \| 'recent_fix'` | Canonical sort keys supported by /ctem/priorities. The server-side ladder for each is in flyto-engine handlers_ctem.go ('sortCTEMPriorities'). Once 'CTEMActionsView' switches to passing '?sort=' and reading the response in order, its local 'sortBy' can be deleted (audit B8, Codex boundary). |
| [`GetCTEMPrioritiesOptions`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/ctem/ctem.ts#L169>) | interface | `export interface GetCTEMPrioritiesOptions { /** Per-team scope filter — empty / undefined = all BUs. */ businessUnitId?: string /** Canonical sort key. Empty / undefined = server default ("priority"). */ sort?: CTEMSortKey /** * Opt-in server-side dedup (audit` | Defines the get ctempriorities options contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |

| [`stopPipelineRun`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L173>) | function | `export function stopPipelineRun(runId: string): Promise<{ ok: boolean; cancelled: boolean }>` | Soft-cancel an in-flight engine-side orchestrator run. |
| [`retestPipelineRun`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L183>) | function | `export function retestPipelineRun(runId: string): Promise<{ ok: boolean run_id: string parent_run_id: string proven_to_test: number started_at: string }>` | Re-fire a completed run's proven findings — used after a fix has shipped to produce a Before/After deliverable. Engine spawns a variant orchestrator that skips baseline+probe. |
| [`openPipelineReportInNewTab`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L203>) | function | `export async function openPipelineReportInNewTab(runId: string): Promise<void>` | Open the customer-facing HTML report in a new tab. The endpoint requires Firebase auth, which window.open() can't provide — so we fetch via authenticated XHR, blob-URL the response, and open that. The blob URL is revoked on tab close. The opened page hosts a "Print → save as PDF" button; users hit Cmd/Ctrl+P from there to produce the SI deliverable. |
| [`getPipelineRun`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L225>) | function | `export function getPipelineRun(runId: string): Promise<PipelineSnapshot>` | Retrieves pipeline run for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`getActivePipelineForCampaign`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L232>) | function | `export function getActivePipelineForCampaign(campaignId: string): Promise<PipelineSnapshot>` | Retrieves active pipeline for campaign for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`CampaignReportResponse`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L251>) | interface | `export interface CampaignReportResponse { ok: boolean markdown: string risk_level?: string generated_at: string error?: string }` | AI-generated executive markdown for a campaign. Endpoint: POST /api/v1/code/campaigns/{id}/report The pipeline finalize step already bakes a summary into the snapshot (PipelineSnapshot.report.executiveSummary), but operators sometimes need a fresh take after threat context shifts or after the original summary was generated with a smaller model. This helper triggers a fresh AI re-generate against the latest execution data. Checks AI quota server-side — returns { ok: false, error } when the org has exceeded its monthly token budget. |
| [`regenerateCampaignReport`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L259>) | function | `export function regenerateCampaignReport(campaignId: string): Promise<CampaignReportResponse>` | Implements regenerate campaign report for the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`fireAndForget`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/pipelineLog.ts#L271>) | function | `export function fireAndForget<T>(label: string, p: Promise<T>): void` | Fire-and-forget helper. Logs to console on failure but never throws — intended for paths where the audit trail is best-effort and a network hiccup must not stall the live pipeline driver. |

Mirrors 'api/report_engine.go:ReportSection'. Mostly consumed by the custom-report export path in 'compounds/reports/buildSections.ts' — built-in templates use 'template_id' instead. |
| [`BuildReportSettings`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/reports/vaReport.ts#L42>) | interface | `export interface BuildReportSettings { report_name?: string description?: string locale?: string classification?: string watermark?: string logo_url?: string include_cover?: boolean include_toc?: boolean }` | Settings envelope — opt-in metadata; backend defaults are sensible enough that omitting 'settings' works for built-in templates. |
| [`BuildReportRequest`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/reports/vaReport.ts#L53>) | interface | `export interface BuildReportRequest { /** Use one OR the other — 'template_id' for backend-registered * built-ins (default / external_ctem / code_audit / executive_summary), * 'sections' for custom reports assembled client-side. */ template_id?: string section` | Defines the build report request contract used by the Flyto2 Engine client. |
| [`buildReport`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/reports/vaReport.ts#L65>) | function | `export function buildReport(orgId: string, req: BuildReportRequest): Promise<Blob>` | POST /reports/build and return the rendered blob. Caller decides whether to render inline (HTML) or trigger a download (PDF). |
| [`buildReportHTML`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/reports/vaReport.ts#L72>) | function | `export async function buildReportHTML(orgId: string, req: Omit<BuildReportRequest, 'format'>): Promise<string>` | Fetch the report HTML as a string for inline iframe preview. Same endpoint as buildReport but unwraps the blob so callers don't have to manage object-URL lifetimes for srcdoc rendering. |
| [`downloadBuiltReport`](<../../src-next/lib/engine/reports/vaReport.ts#L82>) | function | `export async function downloadBuiltReport( orgId: string, req: Omit<BuildReportRequest, 'format'>, filenameHint: string, ): Promise<void>` | Build the report as PDF and trigger a browser download. Falls back to HTML (per the engine — when FLYTO_PDF_URL is unset the server returns HTML with text/html content type instead of failing). The download extension follows the blob's actual mime type so users don't get a '.pdf' file containing HTML. |

| [`setCache`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L120>) | function | `function setCache(key: string, data: Translations): void` | Updates cache for the application. |
| [`loadLocale`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L127>) | function | `async function loadLocale(locale: string): Promise<Translations>` | Retrieves locale for the application. |
| [`detectLocale`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L142>) | function | `function detectLocale(): Locale` | Implements detect locale for the application. |
| [`subscribeLocale`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L192>) | function | `export function subscribeLocale(cb: LocaleListener): () => void` | Subscribe to locale changes. Returns an unsubscribe function. Used by useLocale to drive React re-renders. |
| [`notifyLocaleChange`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L198>) | function | `export function notifyLocaleChange(): void` | Manually fire all locale listeners. Called after setLocale; rarely needed externally. |
| [`getLocaleSnapshot`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L204>) | function | `export function getLocaleSnapshot(): Locale` | Synchronous read of the current locale. Same as getLocale; both kept for ergonomic call sites. |
| [`getLocaleVersionedSnapshot`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L207>) | function | `export function getLocaleVersionedSnapshot(): string` | Versioned snapshot for useSyncExternalStore — changes whenever translations reload, even if locale string stays 'en'. Forces re-render in useLocale. |
| [`getLocale`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L209>) | function | `export function getLocale(): Locale` | Synchronous read of the current locale. |
| [`setLocale`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L212>) | function | `export async function setLocale(locale: Locale): Promise<void>` | Switch locale: load CDN bundle, persist to localStorage, fire listeners. Awaited so callers can await UI re-render. |
| [`isBrokenTranslationValue`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L233>) | function | `function isBrokenTranslationValue(value: string \| undefined): boolean` | Determines whether broken translation value for the application. |
| [`resolveTranslation`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L239>) | function | `function resolveTranslation(key: string): string \| undefined` | Resolves translation for the application. |
| [`t`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L251>) | function | `export function t(key: string, params?: Record<string, string \| number>): string` | Translate 'key' against the active locale. Falls back to en bundle, then to the key itself (so a missing key is visible on screen, not blank). Dev console logs each missing key once for typo detection. Also warns when a key exists only in the en fallback bundle but not in the active CDN translations — this signals a stale bundled baseline. |
| [`tOr`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L278>) | function | `export function tOr(key: string, fallback: string, params?: Record<string, string \| number>): string` | Translate 'key', returning 'fallback' (not the key) when missing from both the active locale AND en. Use this instead of 't(key) \|\| fallback': 't' returns the key on miss, which is truthy, so '\|\| fallback' would never fire while the on-screen string is useless. Adopted for new call sites that prefer an inline English default to creating an en.json entry. |
| [`getAvailableLocales`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L291>) | function | `export function getAvailableLocales()` | Available locales — populated from manifest. No hardcoding. |
| [`init`](<../../src-next/lib/i18n.ts#L300>) | function | `async function init(): Promise<void>` | Implements init for the application. |

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |

| [`FlatTranslations`](<../../src-next/lib/i18nFlatten.ts#L1>) | type | `export type FlatTranslations = Record<string, string>` | Defines the flat translations contract used by the application. |
| [`flattenTranslations`](<../../src-next/lib/i18nFlatten.ts#L12>) | function | `export function flattenTranslations(obj: unknown, prefix = ''): FlatTranslations` | Flatten flyto-i18n nested bundles back into dot keys. flyto-i18n stores parent keys that also have children as: { "parent": { "_self": "Parent label", "child": "Child label" } } Runtime consumers request the bare parent key, so '_self' must resolve to that key rather than to a synthetic child key. |

| [`getGitHubAppInstallURL`](<../../src-next/lib/oauth.ts#L18>) | function | `export function getGitHubAppInstallURL(orgID: string): string \| null` | GitHub App install URL. Reads VITE_GITHUB_APP_SLUG at build time — if unset, the App flow isn't deployed yet, and the caller should fall back to the OAuth path ('connectGitHub'). ## `src-next/lib/queryClient.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`onMutationError`](<../../src-next/lib/queryClient.ts#L14>) | function | `function onMutationError(error: Error, _variables: unknown, _context: unknown, mutation?: Mutation)` | Global mutation error handler — surfaces failures as a DOM event so the SnackbarProvider (or any listener) can show a toast without every mutation needing its own onError. Mutations that already have a local onError will still fire this, but the convention is: local onError handles domain-specific messages, global handler provides the safety net for mutations that forgot. |
| [`onError`](<../../src-next/lib/queryClient.ts#L32>) | object callback | `onError: (error, query) => { const root = Array.isArray(query.queryKey) ? String(query.queryKey[0]) : 'unknown' captureError(error, { queryKey: root, queryHash: query.queryHash }) }` | Handles error for the application. |

## `src-next/lib/queryKeys.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`OrgKey`](<../../src-next/lib/queryKeys.ts#L29>) | type | `type OrgKey<T extends string> = readonly [T, string \| undefined]` | Centralised React Query key factory — domain-grouped. Every consumer that reads org-scoped engine data AND every invalidation site in 'useOrgEvents' must build its key through this module, so a typo on one side can't silently desync from the other. The 'as const' returns make each key a structural tuple type, so a typo becomes a compile error. Adding a new org-scoped read: 1. Add a builder under the right domain here. 2. Use it: 'useQuery({ queryKey: qk.<domain>.<entity>(orgId), ... })' (or a typed hook in 'hooks/engine/'). 3. If an SSE event should refresh it, invalidate the SAME builder in the matching 'useOrgEvents' case. The six original top-level builders (attackSurface, externalPosture, …) are kept FLAT for back-compat — useOrgEvents + ~10 call sites import them as 'qk.externalPosture(orgId)'. Everything else is grouped by domain. Inline string-literal keys are migrated into this factory one domain at a time (see docs/ARCH or the queryKey catalog); 'noAdHocTransport' + future guards keep new code on the factory. Known cache bugs to fix during per-domain migration (read-literal ≠ invalidation-literal): pulse (org-pulse vs pulse), pentest projects (pentest-projects vs pentests), scanSchedule singular/plural, org-issues never invalidated. Builders below pick ONE canonical literal per datum. |
| [`attackSurface`](<../../src-next/lib/queryKeys.ts#L32>) | function | `attackSurface = (o: string \| undefined): OrgKey<'attack-surface'> =>` | Implements attack surface for the application. |
| [`attackSurfaceVariant`](<../../src-next/lib/queryKeys.ts#L33>) | function | `attackSurfaceVariant = (o: string \| undefined, variant: 'confirmed' \| 'with-candidates') =>` | Implements attack surface variant for the application. |
| [`externalPosture`](<../../src-next/lib/queryKeys.ts#L35>) | function | `externalPosture = (o: string \| undefined): OrgKey<'external-posture'> =>` | Implements external posture for the application. |

## `src-next/lib/warroomNav.ts`

| Symbol | Kind | Signature | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`CTEMActionsIntent`](<../../src-next/lib/warroomNav.ts#L19>) | interface | `export interface CTEMActionsIntent { search?: string tiers?: Array<'crown_jewel' \| 'customer_facing' \| 'internal' \| 'sandbox'> severities?: Array<'critical' \| 'high' \| 'medium' \| 'low'> breachedOnly?: boolean verifyingOnly?: boolean hasThreatActor?: boolean un` | Filter intent the operator clicked from somewhere else. CTEM Actions reads this on mount and pre-applies. All fields optional — caller passes only what's relevant to the click. |
| [`navigateToCTEMActions`](<../../src-next/lib/warroomNav.ts#L31>) | function | `export function navigateToCTEMActions(intent?: CTEMActionsIntent)` | Implements navigate to ctemactions for the application. |
| [`consumeCTEMActionsIntent`](<../../src-next/lib/warroomNav.ts#L54>) | function | `export function consumeCTEMActionsIntent(): CTEMActionsIntent \| null` | Read + clear the stashed intent. CTEM Actions calls this once on mount; null when no intent is pending. Audit 2026-05-17: previously removed the key BEFORE parsing, so a malformed payload silently consumed the intent and the caller saw null — the operator's click was lost. The order now is parse → succeed → remove. A parse failure leaves the stash intact so the next consume call retries (and a manual reload surfaces the operator's intent rather than swallowing it). |
| [`navigateToSection`](<../../src-next/lib/warroomNav.ts#L75>) | function | `export function navigateToSection(sectionId: string)` | Generic helper for sections that don't need a filter payload. |
